hardBlocks: Justin Theroux as 'Robert House' is fabricated — Theroux is not in Fallout; Mr. House did not appear as a live cast role in Season 1.
Season 2 'premiered in December 2025' with a '96% Rotten Tomatoes' score and a 'third season confirmed in May 2025' are unverifiable/likely-fabricated future-dated claims.
'$153 million budget' is a specific figure presented as fact without sourcing — likely fabricated precision.
'over 100 million viewers by late 2024' and 'two Creative Arts Emmy wins from 14 nominations' are specific stats presented as fact; the viewer figure and win count are dubious/unsourced.
'94% Rotten Tomatoes' stated as current fact but unverified against cited source.
Internal inconsistency: dek/Takeaways claim '94% Rotten Tomatoes' while body says '93%' — at least one is wrong and both are unverified.

Fabricated Season 2 details: 96% Rotten Tomatoes score, December 2025 premiere, trip to New Vegas, Justin Theroux as Robert House — Season 2 had not aired at any plausible writing date; these are invented.
Fabricated awards specifics: 'won two Creative Arts Emmy Awards out of 14 total nominations' and 'Outstanding Drama Series' nomination presented as fact without sourcing.
Fabricated/uncertain production figure: '$153 million budget' stated as definitive.
Self-dating fabrication: FAQ asserts 'As of mid-2026' and a December 2025 Season 2 debut — speculative future events presented as completed fact.
Season 2 filming locations (LA/Toronto) and Skeleton Coast Namibia claims unverifiable and likely invented.

strengths: Strong scannable structure: BLUF lead, multiple question H2s, key takeaways, FAQ block.
Confident editorial voice with clear verdict and POV framing.
Good keyword placement and meta-style dek; internal and external links present.
Accessible explanation of why the adaptation works for newcomers and fans.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on fabricated/future-tense facts about Season 2 destroys accuracy credibility.
Contradictory RT scores (93% vs 94%) signal sloppy fact control.
External sources are generic (RT, IMDb, Wikipedia) rather than authoritative reporting (Variety, THR) to back specific claims.
Awards and budget numbers stated as fact without attribution.
Information gain undermined because much 'analysis' rests on invented Season 2 content.
No table where one (cast/season comparison) would aid scannability.
